name: Sitara Place Serviced Apartment and Hotel
description: Sitara Place Hotel & Serviced Apartment is a family run business. Conveniently located in the local neighbourhood of Ratchada soi 3 with lots of delicious eateries close by. Fully furnished 39 sqm units. Wifi & Cable TV. Kitchenette with Fridge and Microwave. King Size bed. Gym. Parking. Free Tuk Tuk to MRT Phra Ram 9 during the day. 10 minute walk from Phra Ram 9 MRT Station, Malls and lots more. We hope that our guests leave Bangkok with warm memories of their stay with us.
neighborhood_overview: The Yoo Charoen neighbourhood in Ratchada Soi 3 where Sitara Place is located is a bustling local area. Lots of yummy local food. Lots of conveniences close by. We are a hop and skip away from the JODD Fairs Night Market and The ONE night market which are full of good eats and music for the evenings. Must try the Phad Thai vendor who opens at 5pm around the corner. 10 minute walk from MRT Rama 9 which connects to the BTS sky train and Airport Link train.  We are behind the Chinese Embassy, walking distance from Fortune IT Mall and Central Grand Rama 9.

name: Canal House Bangkok - Whole house on Mon canal
description:
Since the house is located right on the canal, you’ll experience the beauty of living by the canal, including stunning sunsets🌅

However ⚠️please note that boat noise is present from 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM. It’s all part of the authentic riverside experience!

Entire antique canal house located on Mon canal on the Thonburi side (old capital city) of Bangkok.

Walking distance to:
❤ Itsaraphab MRT subway - 15mins (walk)
★Wat Arun - 10 mins
🙏 Wat Pho - 15 mins
★Grand Palace- 20 mins
neighborhood_overview: The house sits on stilts on the canal, just like traditional Thai houses. From 7am-7pm you'll see longtail boats passing by, offering a fantastic opportunity to watch local life. In our backyard is a local temple called “Wat nakklang” . There are two elementary schools inside the temple so in the morning you will experience school life. In the evening, you'll get to see the daily outdoor aerobic dance workout - feel free to join them! There are plenty of good local restaurants within walking distance and you will eat like a local.

name: BaanYok, Unique 1920s Home in Chinatown
description: Stay in a fully restored 1920s Chinese-Portuguese shophouse in the heart of Bangkok’s most vibrant neighborhood: Soi Nana, Chinatown. This unique 2-story home is full of character, original details, and local soul. Surrounded by spice shops, design bars, street food, and history, it’s perfect for travelers seeking an authentic, stylish, and memorable Bangkok experience. Walk to metro, ferry, and explore the city from a truly special place.
neighborhood_overview:
The house is located in the heart of Chinatown, one of the most authentic neighborhoods in Bangkok. A district well known for still preserving its original essence and old traditions, if you are staying in my house you are bound to experience up close the charming old city life.

Soi Nana, the street where my house is situated, is undoubtedly one of the trendiest places in the city, with cool bars, cafes and art spaces attracting many local visitors.

Apart from that, you will find many other interesting spots – including temples, markets, museums, etc- as well as other hidden-places to be found during the day and after sunset, definitely the best street food in town around Yaowarat Road, all within walking distance from the house.

description: Discover your rooftop haven in bustling Bangkok! This quiet, cozy EnSuite is nestled in Sathon, a prime location just minutes from the subway, vibrant Silom, and tranquil Lumpini Park. neighborhood_overview:
Discover the authentic heart of Bangkok in the vibrant Sathon neighborhood. Like Sukhumvit, Sathon offers local treasures such as budget-friendly food courts and street food ($2-3 USD per meal), traditional Thai massages, and a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

Quickly access Silom Road and Sukhumvit Road via metro or motorcycle taxi ($1-2 USD per trip). Sathon's rich infrastructure provides 5-star hotel-like comfort at a fraction of the cost. Stay a week and enjoy Lumpini Park's free yoga, tai chi, aerobics, and jogging.

Recommended experiences for guests:

Morning & evening Thai food courts
Massage, yoga classes, traditional Chinese medicine clinics
Weekend markets & Silom night market

neighborhood_overview: Banglampoo, chatter and movement flow outward from Khaosan Road, an ever-evolving marketplace and bar scene for travelers on the Southeast Asia circuit. Although the incandescent path is Banglampoo's most recognizable landmark, there's more to this north Bangkok neighborhood than fish spas and henna tattoos. Banglampoo is just as much made from what you don't immediately see—locally famous noodle shops operate from apartment buildings' bottom floors and temples tuck into almost-quiet side streets.

name: Tranquil Double Room in Hampstead
description:
Extremely private & quiet double room with ensuite bathroom in Grade II listed Victorian lodge situated in a secluded gated enclave with extensive gardens.
A short walk to bustling village, tube station, & Hampstead Heath's 800 acres of parkland.
neighborhood_overview:
The Lodge is a Grade II listed building located on a private communal garden at the centre of a gated enclave of housing built in the late C19th on the former site of Hampstead Wells spa.

In 1698, six acres of swampy ground that contained chalybeate water were donated to the 'poor of Hampstead' by the then Lords of the Manor, the Gainsborough family. Hampstead Wells was a popular spa in the early C18th and contributed to the area's development as a fashionable place of residence.

The houses and communal garden were laid out by the Wells and Campden Charity from the 1880s, covering part of the former pleasure grounds where a bowling green and ornamental pond had been, the site of the latter visible in the lawn. Today the lawns are surrounded by shrubberies with a wide variety of plants and mature trees. A path runs round the edge with a number of small pergolas marking entrances to the gardens.

neighborhood_overview:
HOW CENTRAL?
This flat is in Zone 1. London’s official centre! The flat is closer to Edgware Road than shown on the map. We’re located between two completely contrasting neighbourhoods: The busy Edgware Road, where you can get the freshest, best kebabs at any time of night or shop for bargain fruit and veg, at a real London street Market and sample real street food (as opposed to expensive Hipster Street food). Walk in the opposite direction and you’ll find: Little Venice prettily perched on Regents Canal (and part of a wonderful canal walk), Little Venice is home to London’s chattering classes. This place is a wonderful crossroads and a great place to explore London from for the curious.

neighborhood_overview:
An upmarket residential area at the foot of Vlakkenberg Mountain Range, part of the Table Mountain National Park. Close to convenience shops, restaurants, and the beach. The beautiful coastal drive to Cape Town takes about 20 minutes.

Hout Bay is a charming coastal town located not far from Cape Town. Known for its picturesque landscapes and laid-back atmosphere, it is a popular destination for both locals and tourists alike. With its stunning beaches, lush mountains, and rich cultural heritage, Hout Bay offers a unique experience to those who visit.
The town's name, Hout Bay, translates to "Wood Bay" in English, and it is said to have originated from the abundance of timber that was once found in the area. Today, the town is better known for its fishing industry, which has been a mainstay of the local economy for centuries. Visitors to Hout Bay can still see traditional fishing boats in the harbor, and can sample some of the freshest seafood in the region.

Klein Slangkop Private Estate:
Our guests love the fact that our Estate has direct access onto pristine white sandy beaches with turquoise seas. The estate is a natural beach setting where we sometimes spot little tortoises ambling about. We’ve been very lucky to get the rare midnight visits from a magnificent porcupine. Whales visit our bay and the bird life is prolific. We are a stroll away from Long Beach, one of the most popular surf beaches in Cape Town. You’ll find that nature, surf boards, skate boards, bicycles and prams take priority on the estate.

Kommetjie:
There is lots to see and do in the friendly village of Kommetjie. There are beautiful hiking trails to enjoy nature. Cycling around the village is easy going. You could cycle to the famous lighthouse or local deli to buy freshly baked bread and croissants, stop for a good cup of coffee and chat at one of the local cafés. The community of Kommetjie is warm and helpful.

Cradled between the city and the V&A Waterfront, De Waterkant Village is avant-garde, a cultural urban refuge highlighted by the abundance of restaurants, art galleries, clubs, salsa clubs, tapas bars and Parisian inspired café's. Drawing comparisons from cultural and artistic villages from all over the world, De Waterkant embraces individuality and theatricality, echoed in the architecture and vibrancy of its residents and visitors.

name: Camps Bay studio apartment with magnificent views.
description:
Wake up to birdsong and the sound of the ocean.
An architectural award winning property, situated in a quiet cul-de-sac at the foot of Table Mountain, bordering the Table Mountain Nature reserve, with magnificent views of the Atlantic Ocean, this gorgeous little apartment is a piece of paradise.

10 minutes drive from the city centre and a 5 minute drive to the beach, it’s ideally positioned to explore Cape Town’s main attractions. Perfect for nature lovers, hikers and beachcombers.
neighborhood_overview:
Camps Bay is one of the most beautiful suburbs in the country, famous for its magnificent beaches, views and mountain access. Our property, set high above the often hectically busy beachfront and surrounded by nature, belies the fact that you are within minutes of one of South Africa’s biggest cities.
Table Mountain National Park borders our property with many beautiful hiking routes to choose from. It is possible to hike to the top of Table Mountain or Lions Head straight from the apartment.
